title: Rhomboda atrorubens
intro: Rhomboda atrorubens, also known as the Red-rimmed Rhomboda, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rhomboda atrorubens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Rhomboda atrorubens is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Rhomboda is a part of the subtribe Coelogyneinae. Rhomboda atrorubens is native to the Himalayas. Its classification places it within the kingdom Plantae.
